title: astragalus tyghensis
intro: Astragalus tyghensis, also known as Tygh Valley milkvetch, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It is native to the Tygh Valley in Oregon, USA, and has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, and is used to boost the immune system, reduce fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Astragalus tyghensis is typically prepared as a decoction, capsule, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance its health benefits.
